L. 109–280, title II, § 202(f)120 Stat. 885Aug. 17, 2006“(1).—In generalThe amendments made by this section [enacting and amending this section and ] shall apply with respect to plan years beginning after 2007.section 1085 of this titlesection 1132 of this title“(2).—Special rule for certain noticesIn any case in which a plan’s actuary certifies that it is reasonably expected that a multiemployer plan will be in critical status under section 305(b)(3) of the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974 [], as added by this section, with respect to the first plan year beginning after 2007, the notice required under subparagraph (D) of such section may be provided at any time after the date of enactment [], so long as it is provided on or before the last date for providing the notice under such subparagraph.29 U.S.C. 1085(b)(3)Aug. 17, 2006“(3).—Special rule for certain restored benefitsIn the case of a multiemployer plan—“(A)with respect to which benefits were reduced pursuant to a plan amendment adopted on or after , and before , andJanuary 1, 2002June 30, 2005“(B)which, pursuant to the plan document, the trust agreement, or a formal written communication from the plan sponsor to participants provided before , provided for the restoration of such benefits,June 30, 2005the amendments made by this section shall not apply to such benefit restorations to the extent that any restriction on the providing or accrual of such benefits would otherwise apply by reason of such amendments.”

children.heading: Requirement to meet minimum funding standard
children.label: a Requirement to meet minimum funding standard

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: A plan to which this part applies shall satisfy the minimum funding standard applicable to the plan for any plan year.

children.label: A
children.text: in the case of a defined benefit plan which is a single-employer plan (other than a CSEC plan), the employer makes contributions to or under the plan for the plan year which, in the aggregate, are not less than the minimum required contribution determined under for the plan for the plan year,section 1083 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: in the case of a money purchase plan which is a single-employer plan, the employer makes contributions to or under the plan for the plan year which are required under the terms of the plan,

children.label: C
children.text: in the case of a multiemployer plan, the employers make contributions to or under the plan for any plan year which, in the aggregate, are sufficient to ensure that the plan does not have an accumulated funding deficiency under as of the end of the plan year, andsection 1084 of this title

children.label: D
children.text: in the case of a CSEC plan, the employers make contributions to or under the plan for any plan year which, in the aggregate, are sufficient to ensure that the plan does not have an accumulated funding deficiency under as of the end of the plan year.section 1085a of this title

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (2), the amount of any contribution required by this section (including any required installments under paragraphs (3) and (4) of or under ) shall be paid by the employer responsible for making contributions to or under the plan.section 1083(j) of this titlesection 1085a(f) of this title

children.heading: Joint and several liability where employer member of controlled group
children.label: 2 Joint and several liability where employer member of controlled group
children.text: If the employer referred to in paragraph (1) is a member of a controlled group, each member of such group shall be jointly and severally liable for payment of such contributions.

children.heading: Multiemployer plans in critical status
children.label: 3 Multiemployer plans in critical status
children.text: Paragraph (1) shall not apply in the case of a multiemployer plan for any plan year in which the plan is in critical status pursuant to . This paragraph shall only apply if the plan sponsor adopts a rehabilitation plan in accordance with and complies with the terms of such rehabilitation plan (and any updates or modifications of the plan).section 1085 of this titlesection 1085(e) of this title

children.heading: Variance from minimum funding standards
children.label: c Variance from minimum funding standards

children.heading: Waiver in case of business hardship
children.label: 1 Waiver in case of business hardship
i
children.text: an employer is (or in the case of a multiemployer plan or a CSEC plan, 10 percent or more of the number of employers contributing to or under the plan are) unable to satisfy the minimum funding standard for a plan year without temporary substantial business hardship (substantial business hardship in the case of a multiemployer plan), and

children.label: ii
children.text: application of the standard would be adverse to the interests of plan participants in the aggregate,

children.label: i
children.text: in the case of a single-employer plan (other than a CSEC plan), the minimum required contribution under for the plan year shall be reduced by the amount of the waived funding deficiency and such amount shall be amortized as required under ,section 1083 of this titlesection 1083(e) of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: in the case of a multiemployer plan, the funding standard account shall be credited under with the amount of the waived funding deficiency and such amount shall be amortized as required under , andsection 1084(b)(3)(C) of this titlesection 1084(b)(2)(C) of this title

children.label: iii
children.text: in the case of a CSEC plan, the funding standard account shall be credited under with the amount of the waived funding deficiency and such amount shall be amortized as required under .section 1085a(b)(3)(C) of this titlesection 1085a(b)(2)(C) of this title

children.heading: Waiver of amortized portion not allowed
children.label: C Waiver of amortized portion not allowed
children.text: The Secretary of the Treasury may not waive under subparagraph (A) any portion of the minimum funding standard under subsection (a) for a plan year which is attributable to any waived funding deficiency for any preceding plan year.

children.label: A
children.text: the employer is operating at an economic loss,

children.label: B
children.text: there is substantial unemployment or underemployment in the trade or business and in the industry concerned,

children.label: C
children.text: the sales and profits of the industry concerned are depressed or declining, and

children.label: D
children.text: it is reasonable to expect that the plan will be continued only if the waiver is granted.

children.heading: Waived funding deficiency
children.label: 3 Waived funding deficiency
children.text: For purposes of this part, the term “waived funding deficiency” means the portion of the minimum funding standard under subsection (a) (determined without regard to the waiver) for a plan year waived by the Secretary of the Treasury and not satisfied by employer contributions.

children.heading: Security for waivers for single-employer plans, consultations
children.label: 4 Security for waivers for single-employer plans, consultations

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (C), the Secretary of the Treasury may require an employer maintaining a defined benefit plan which is a single-employer plan (within the meaning of ) to provide security to such plan as a condition for granting or modifying a waiver under paragraph (1) or for granting an extension under .section 1301(a)(15) of this titlesection 1085a(d) of this title

children.heading: Special rules
children.label: ii Special rules
children.text: Any security provided under clause (i) may be perfected and enforced only by the Pension Benefit Guaranty Corporation, or at the direction of the Corporation, by a contributing sponsor (within the meaning of ), or a member of such sponsor’s controlled group (within the meaning of ).section 1301(a)(13) of this titlesection 1301(a)(14) of this title

children.heading: Consultation with the Pension Benefit Guaranty Corporation
children.label: B Consultation with the Pension Benefit Guaranty Corporation
I
children.text: notice of the completed application for any waiver, modification, or extension, and

children.label: II
children.text: an opportunity to comment on such application within 30 days after receipt of such notice, and

children.label: I
children.text: any comments of the Corporation under clause (i)(II), and

children.label: II
children.text: any views of any employee organization (within the meaning of ) representing participants in the plan which are submitted in writing to the Secretary of the Treasury in connection with such application.section 1002(4) of this title

children.heading: Exception for certain waivers or extensions
children.label: C Exception for certain waivers or extensions
I
children.text: the aggregate unpaid minimum required contributions for the plan year and all preceding plan years, or the accumulated funding deficiency under , whichever is applicable,section 1085a of this title

children.label: II
children.text: the present value of all waiver amortization installments determined for the plan year and succeeding plan years under section 1083(e)(2) or 1085a(b)(2)(C) of this title, whichever is applicable, and

children.label: III
children.text: the total amounts not paid by reason of an extension in effect under ,section 1085a(d) of this title

children.heading: Treatment of waivers or extensions for which applications are pending
children.label: ii Treatment of waivers or extensions for which applications are pending
children.text: The amount described in clause (i)(I) shall include any increase in such amount which would result if all applications for waivers or extensions with respect to the minimum funding standard under this subsection which are pending with respect to such plan were denied.

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: The term “unpaid minimum required contribution” means, with respect to any plan year, any minimum required contribution under for the plan year which is not paid on or before the due date (as determined under ) for the plan year.section 1083 of this titlesection 1083(j)(1) of this title

children.heading: Ordering rule
children.label: II Ordering rule
children.text: For purposes of subclause (I), any payment to or under a plan for any plan year shall be allocated first to unpaid minimum required contributions for all preceding plan years on a first-in, first-out basis and then to the minimum required contribution under for the plan year.section 1083 of this title

children.heading: Application must be submitted before date 2½ months after close of year
children.label: A Application must be submitted before date 2½ months after close of year
children.text: In the case of a single-employer plan, no waiver may be granted under this subsection with respect to any plan for any plan year unless an application therefor is submitted to the Secretary of the Treasury not later than the 15th day of the 3rd month beginning after the close of such plan year.

children.heading: Special rule if employer is member of controlled group
children.label: B Special rule if employer is member of controlled group
i
children.text: with respect to such employer, and

children.label: ii
children.text: with respect to the controlled group of which such employer is a member (determined by treating all members of such group as a single employer).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary of the Treasury shall, before granting a waiver under this subsection, require each applicant to provide evidence satisfactory to such Secretary that the applicant has provided notice of the filing of the application for such waiver to each affected party (as defined in ). Such notice shall include a description of the extent to which the plan is funded for benefits which are guaranteed under subchapter III and for benefit liabilities.section 1301(a)(21) of this title

children.heading: Consideration of relevant information
children.label: B Consideration of relevant information
children.text: The Secretary of the Treasury shall consider any relevant information provided by a person to whom notice was given under subparagraph (A).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: No amendment of a plan which increases the liabilities of the plan by reason of any increase in benefits, any change in the accrual of benefits, or any change in the rate at which benefits become nonforfeitable under the plan shall be adopted if a waiver under this subsection or an extension of time under or is in effect with respect to the plan, or if a plan amendment described in subsection (d)(2) which reduces the accrued benefit of any participant has been made at any time in the preceding 12 months (24 months in the case of a multiemployer plan). If a plan is amended in violation of the preceding sentence, any such waiver, or extension of time, shall not apply to any plan year ending on or after the date on which such amendment is adopted.section 1084(d) of this titlesection 1085a(d) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: the Secretary of the Treasury determines to be reasonable and which provides for only de minimis increases in the liabilities of the plan,

children.label: ii
children.text: only repeals an amendment described in subsection (d)(2), or

children.label: iii
children.text: is required as a condition of qualification under part I of subchapter D of chapter 1 of title 26.

children.heading: Cross reference
children.label: 8 Cross reference
children.text: For corresponding duties of the Secretary of the Treasury with regard to implementation of title 26, see .section 412(c) of title 26

children.heading: Change in method or year
children.label: 1 Change in method or year
children.text: If the funding method or a plan year for a plan is changed, the change shall take effect only if approved by the Secretary of the Treasury.

children.label: A
children.text: is adopted after the close of such plan year but no later than 2½ months after the close of the plan year (or, in the case of a multiemployer plan, no later than 2 years after the close of such plan year),

children.label: B
children.text: does not reduce the accrued benefit of any participant determined as of the beginning of the first plan year to which the amendment applies, and

children.label: C
children.text: does not reduce the accrued benefit of any participant determined as of the time of adoption except to the extent required by the circumstances,

children.heading: Controlled group
children.label: 3 Controlled group
children.text: For purposes of this section, the term “controlled group” means any group treated as a single employer under subsection (b), (c), (m), or () of .osection 414 of title 26
